questions.question: In the `get_orders` action of `amazon/ajax_load.php`, what value is used for the SP-API `LastUpdatedAfter` parameter?

options.text: The timestamp of the last successfully synced order
The current time minus 16 minutes
Midnight of the current day
The value stored in `TBL_AZ_REPORT.last_sync_time`

questions.question: When a new Amazon order is inserted by `get_orders`, what happens to the `amazon_reserved` column in `TBL_PRODUCTS_MASTER`?

options.text: It is incremented by the order quantity
It is decremented (reduced) for the matching product
It is set to zero
It is not affected — amazon_reserved is managed by a separate cron job

questions.question: How does the `update_orders` action detect that an AFN order should be auto-marked as shipped?

options.text: By checking `TBL_AZ_ORDERS.shipmentStatus = 'delivered'`
When Amazon's SP-API returns `az_status='Shipped'` but the local `status` is still `new`
By reading the `TBL_FULFILLMENT.fulfillmentStatus` field
Through a webhook notification from Amazon

questions.question: How are AFN (FBA) return orders identified in `TBL_AZ_ORDERS`?

options.text: By `fulfillmentChannel = 'AFN_RETURN'`
By `isAFNReturns = 1` and an order ID prefixed with `S02-`
By `isReplacementOrder = 1`
Through a `returnType` column set to `'afn'`

questions.question: In the SP-API connector class, how is the OAuth2 access token managed?

options.text: A new token is requested for every API call
The token is auto-refreshed with a 60-second offset before expiry, and stored in `TBL_AZ_ACCOUNTS` (`access_token` and `token_expiry` columns)
The token is stored in PHP session variables and refreshed on page load
Token management is handled by the MWS client library

questions.question: Which operations still use the deprecated MWS API (`MCS\MWSClient`) rather than SP-API?

options.text: GetOrders, GetOrder, and ListOrderItems
RequestOrderLabel (label generation), GetFeedSubmissionResult (label PDF download), and GetReport (AFN data/tracking)
All payment reconciliation operations
Only the order cancellation flow

questions.question: What is the correct sequence for the EasyShip label generation flow in the Amazon module?

options.text: `get_labels` → `request_labels` → `schedule_pickup`
`schedule_pickup` (SP-API ListPickupSlots + MWS RequestOrderLabel) → `request_labels` (MWS RequestOrderLabel) → `get_labels` (MWS GetFeedSubmissionResult → PDF) → `generate_labels` (mPDF merge)
`generate_labels` → `schedule_pickup` → `get_labels`
`request_labels` → `schedule_pickup` → `generate_labels` → `get_labels`

questions.question: In the `scan_ship` action of `ajax_load.php`, what data preparation occurs before the operator scans UIDs?

options.text: Only the order details are fetched from `TBL_AZ_ORDERS`
RTD order items are loaded by tracking_id, combo products are resolved via `TBL_PRODUCTS_COMBO`, and "Order Process Start" is logged to `TBL_PROCESS_LOG`
A new fulfilment record is created in `TBL_FULFILLMENT`
The SP-API is called to verify the order is still active

questions.question: In the `save_scan_ship` action, what operations occur per UID after the operator submits scanned items?

options.text: Only `TBL_AZ_ORDERS.uid` is updated with the UID JSON array
`$stockist->update_inventory_status($uid, 'sales')` + `$stockist->add_inventory_log($uid, 'sales', 'Amazon Sales :: orderId')` + "Order Process End" logged to `TBL_PROCESS_LOG`
The UID is written directly to `TBL_INVENTORY.inv_status = 'shipped'`
`$stockist->update_stock_on_mps()` is called to update Amazon stock levels

questions.question: In `flex_process_request.php`, what CORS restriction controls access to the Flex endpoint?

options.text: A token-based authentication via `AuthMiddleware`
`$_SERVER['HTTP_ORIGIN']` must be `https://sellerflex.amazon.in` or `https://beta-sellerflex.amazon.in`
The request must include an Amazon API signature header
IP allowlisting from Amazon's CDN

questions.question: In the `add_details` action of `flex_process_request.php`, what distinguishes a Flex-processed order in `TBL_AZ_ORDERS`?

options.text: `fulfillmentChannel = 'FLEX'` and `status = 'flex_shipped'`
`fulfilmentSite = 'ZWCI'`, `courierName = 'ATS'`, and the order is processed by BOT user (userID=86, role='bot')
`shipmentStatus = 'flex_complete'` and `isFlexOrder = 1`
A separate `TBL_AZ_FLEX_ORDERS` table is used

questions.question: In `functions.php`, what does `update_returns_status()` do when a return is marked as "received"?

options.text: Creates a Safe-T claim and completes the return
Sets `rReceivedDate` on the return record and withdraws any undelivered claims
Immediately routes the returned UIDs to `qc_pending` via `$stockist`
Triggers a refund to the customer via Amazon's API

questions.question: When a return is marked as "completed" in `update_returns_status()`, what inventory operation occurs per UID?

options.text: `$stockist->update_inventory_status($uid, 'sales')` — marking it as sold
`$stockist->update_inventory_status($uid, 'qc_pending')` + `$stockist->add_inventory_log($uid, 'sales_return', ...)` — routing the item back to the QAQC pipeline
The UID is deleted from `TBL_INVENTORY`
`$stockist->update_inventory_status($uid, 'inbound')` — treating it as new stock

questions.question: What is a "removal order" in the Amazon module, and which table tracks it?

options.text: An order cancelled by the customer, tracked in `TBL_AZ_ORDERS` with `status='cancelled'`

FBA inventory being returned from Amazon's fulfillment centres to the seller, tracked in `TBL_AZ_REMOVAL_ORDERS` with fields like `fnsku` and status progression (return_pending → in_transit → received → completed)
A product removed from Amazon's catalogue, tracked in `TBL_AZ_PRODUCTS`
A return order that was rejected, tracked in `TBL_AZ_CLAIMS`

questions.question: In the `mark_orders > return_completed` sub-case of `ajax_load.php`, what validation occurs before completing a return?

options.text: The system checks if a Safe-T claim exists for the return
The scanned UIDs are compared against the stored UIDs in the order to verify they match
The Amazon SP-API is called to confirm the return status
The refund amount is validated against the original order total

questions.question: How does `AmazonPayments->get_marketplace_charges()` determine which fee calculation methods to call?

options.text: It uses a hardcoded list of fee functions in the class constructor
It reads `TBL_FEES_CHART` for charge definitions (forward/return/replacement/RTO) and dynamically calls `$this->$function($order)` for each fee type mapped in the chart
It reads the fee list from the Amazon SP-API
It uses a configuration file stored in the `/assets/templates/` directory

questions.question: During settlement CSV import via `insert_payments()`, how are Amazon's varying column names handled?

options.text: Column names are hardcoded in the PHP file
The `get_option('amazon_payments_key_mapping')` option provides column name normalisation, and any unrecognised column keys trigger an insert into `TBL_DEV_NOTIFICATIONS` to alert developers
A separate mapping table `TBL_AZ_COLUMN_MAP` stores the translations
The CSV headers are parsed and auto-matched using fuzzy string comparison

questions.question: What does the `get_all_unsettled` payment action automatically do for orders with a settlement difference of ₹0.50 or less?

options.text: Flags the order for manual review
Automatically marks the order as settled (considers the difference negligible)
Creates a dispute record in `TBL_AZ_INCIDENTS`
Sends a notification to the seller about the discrepancy

questions.question: How does the Amazon module handle stock release when an order is cancelled, compared to Flipkart?

options.text: Both use a database trigger to automatically release stock
Amazon relies on PHP-level `update_order_inventory_status()` called during `get_orders` sync, whereas Flipkart uses the `trg_before_fk_status_update` database trigger for automatic stock release
Amazon uses a webhook to trigger stock release; Flipkart uses a cron job
Neither module handles stock release — it is managed by the inventory module's own cron

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the data synchronisation model difference between Amazon and Shopify modules?

options.text: Both use webhook-based push models but with different authentication

Amazon uses a cron-based pull model (`get_orders` with SP-API, no webhook receiver), while Shopify uses a push model with 11 webhook event types across 3 authentication strategies (Shopify HMAC, Razorpay HMAC, unauthenticated GoKwik/Easebuzz)
Amazon uses webhooks for orders but pulls payments; Shopify does the reverse
Both use identical cron-based pull models with 15-minute intervals
